sections.paragraph:
ἀνεξίκακος, ον (s. ἀνέχομαι, κακός; Lucian, Jud. Voc. 9; Vett. Val. 38, 21; Cat. Cod. Astr. VIII/2 p. 156, 15; Pollux 5, 138; PTebt 272, 19 [II a.d.]; Just., A. I, 16, 1; Ath. 34, 2.—ἀνεξικακία Epict., Ench. 10; Wsd 2:19; Jos., Bell. 1, 624; perh. also Acta Alex. X, 40f: [ἔγνω]|σται μὲν ἡ ἐμ[ὴ ἄνεξι]|κακία) pert. to bearing evil without resentment, patient, tolerant of Christians 2 Ti 2:24 (w. ἤπιος, διδακτικός).—TW.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

perh. perh. = perhaps

Acta Alex. Acta Alex. = Acta Alexandrinorum—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
